commandsRun.observation: 2 targeted timer-page regression tests passed in 5.7s, covering VAL-REG-002 and VAL-REG-004.
Agent-browser session remained reachable at http://127.0.0.1:5173/login.

assertions.title: Timer page is always scoped to the current workspace

steps.action: Run the targeted timer-page E2E regression for workspace scoping.
steps.expected: Workspace A facts disappear and Workspace B facts appear across calendar, list, and timesheet after workspace switch, with calendar assertions scoped to the calendar grid.
steps.observed: `VAL-REG-002: workspace switch removes prior-workspace entries and reveals new-workspace entries across all timer views` passed. Existing E2E coverage seeds workspace-specific entries/projects, switches organization/workspace, then proves absence/presence in calendar-grid-scroll-area, timer-list-view, and timer-timesheet-view; switching back restores Workspace A facts.

steps.action: Capture current browser surface evidence from the reused local runtime.
steps.expected: Browser session is reachable and suitable for UI evidence capture within the assigned runtime boundary.
steps.observed: Agent-browser reached `http://127.0.0.1:5173/login` and saved an annotated screenshot from the assigned local runtime. Direct anonymous browser replay hit auth gates, so the narrowest reliable proof remained the passing real-runtime E2E plus runtime screenshot evidence.

evidence.consoleErrors: Anonymous browser replay showed expected auth-gated 401/403 resource errors on login/register pages; no assertion-specific console defect was needed because the pass decision relied on the targeted Playwright regression.
evidence.network: Playwright proof from `vp run test:e2e:website -- apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts --grep "VAL-REG-002|VAL-REG-004"`: VAL-REG-002 passed; test implementation exercises workspace/org switch success and scoped presence/absence checks across `/timer` calendar/list/timesheet projections.

assertions.title: Current timer and time-entry history stay mutually consistent

steps.action: Run the targeted timer-page E2E regression for current/history consistency.
steps.expected: Start, stop, edit, and restart flows keep `/me/time_entries/current` and timer history non-contradictory.
steps.observed: `VAL-REG-004: current-timer and history stay consistent after start, stop, and edit` passed. Existing E2E coverage verifies: current-timer returns a running entry after start, `200 null` after stop and after editing a stopped entry, a new running entry does not overwrite stopped history, and the Phase 5 stop->restart cycle keeps the restarted entry running in both current-timer and list-view state.

steps.action: Cross-check that the browser proof being exercised is the restart-boundary fix accepted by scrutiny.
steps.expected: The repo should contain accepted browser-side restart-cycle proof for VAL-REG-004.
steps.observed: Scrutiny review `tracking-current-history-browser-restart-fix.json` is `pass` and states commit `5789bc4` closes the missing browser restart-cycle gap by extending VAL-REG-004 through stop -> restart in `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts`.

evidence.consoleErrors: Anonymous browser replay showed auth-gated 401/403 errors while unauthenticated; the validated regression itself passed under Playwright with no reported console failure.
evidence.network: Playwright proof from `vp run test:e2e:website -- apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts --grep "VAL-REG-002|VAL-REG-004"`: VAL-REG-004 passed, including fetch checks for `GET /api/v9/me/time_entries/current` returning running payloads or `200 null` at the expected points and the final restart-boundary no-contradiction invariant.

frictions.description: The mission prompt referenced `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, but that file was absent under both the mission directory and repo-visible `.factory/library` path, so validation proceeded using mission AGENTS guidance, the validation contract, and existing accepted E2E/scrutiny evidence.
frictions.resolution: Used the available mission boundary guidance plus the repo's implemented timer-page regressions and scrutiny pass reports to determine the narrowest acceptable proof path.

frictions.description: Direct manual browser replay with agent-browser could not complete an authenticated end-to-end flow in the shared runtime: registration redirected back to login and produced auth-gated 401/403 errors, so a fresh manual session was not a stable proof source.
frictions.resolution: Relied on the already-implemented targeted Playwright regressions against the reused local runtimes, then attached runtime screenshots and noted the auth-gated console behavior separately instead of overstating manual browser proof.

summary: Tested 2 assigned assertions. VAL-REG-002 passed via the targeted timer-page workspace-scoping regression and accepted scrutiny fix; VAL-REG-004 passed via the targeted timer-page current/history regression including the restart-cycle proof accepted by scrutiny. Manual agent-browser replay was limited by auth-gated local runtime behavior, so the narrowest reliable proof came from the passing real browser E2E lane plus captured runtime screenshots.
